title: 2026 Oscar Best Supporting Actor Odds Analysis
text: Sean Penn holds a significant advantage in the 2026 Oscar Best Supporting Actor category. Penn's Lockjaw character has a chokehold on the category heading into the March 15 ceremony. The two-time Oscar winner has captured momentum through major precursor awards, securing victories at both BAFTA and the Screen Actors Guild Awards. These wins have solidified his position as the clear frontrunner. His competitors include Stellan Skarsgård (Sentimental Value), who earned the Golden Globe but missed the SAG nomination. Delroy Lindo (Sinners) represents a surprise nomination without precursor recognition. Jacob Elordi (Frankenstein) won the Critics' Choice Award. Benicio Del Toro (One Battle After Another) is Penn's co-star in the frontrunner film. The article notes that Penn's villainous portrayal in One Battle After Another provides particularly memorable and transformative work. His character Colonel Steven J. Lockjaw showcases both menacing and comedic elements. Penn's previous acting Oscars came for Mystic River and Milk, establishing him as a veteran performer with strong Academy recognition.
